findings.description: Rule conflates two distinct governance concerns: ALL CAPS casing requirement and typeface/medium specification (Agenda for print vs Nunito Sans Bold+ for email fallback). These should be split into a casing rule and a typography/font rule with medium-specific scope, or the font requirement should be captured as a separate scoped facet.

new_rules.scope: print: Agenda; email fallback: Nunito Sans Bold+

findings.description: Source explicitly cross-references '(see 1.6)' indicating this rule is governed by or duplicated from another section. This governance/provenance link is not captured on the rule.

Source item is explicitly tagged [GENERAL] and phrased as 'other than the cases above,' signaling it is an exception-governed general rule dependent on the campaign headline and chart headline ALL CAPS rules. No relation/exception facet or governance tag is captured linking this rule to those exceptions.

Source text ('Body copy: sentence case.') is a plain declarative statement with no modal/emphasis markers (no bold, no 'must', no 'always'), unlike the ALL CAPS rules which use explicit emphasis. Hardness of hard_constraint appears overstated versus the sibling strong_default headline rule; should likely be strong_default.

Missing explicit relation modeling: the [GENERAL] no-ALL-CAPS rule is the default and the campaign/chart headline rules are its carve-out exceptions; this hierarchical relation is not represented anywhere in the rule set, risking future rules being added without honoring the exception scope.
